ogTitle: Surfside vs Sussex Inlet: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Surfside and Sussex Inlet.
heading: Surfside vs Sussex Inlet — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Surfside, New South Wales and Sussex Inlet, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Sussex Inlet has a lower overall climate risk score (44/100) compared to Surfside (44/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Surfside or Sussex Inlet safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Surfside has a flood risk score of 54/100, while Sussex Inlet scores 60/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Surfside scores 61/100 for bushfire risk. Sussex Inlet scores 31/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Surfside or Sussex Inlet?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Sussex Inlet has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.15% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
